title: carlina corymbosa var. lobeliana
intro: Carlina corymbosa var. lobeliana, also known as Lobel's carline thistle, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a member of the sunflower family and has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or inflorescence to be used in herbal remedies, and it is also used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
